steps.q: Is the leisure battery above 12 V?
steps.no: Charge and test the leisure battery. Inspect the main battery fuse and terminals.

steps.q: Is battery voltage present at the Calira EVS battery connection?
steps.no: Trace the battery cable, main fuse and earth return.

steps.q: Is battery voltage present at the VB06 input?
steps.no: Inspect the EVS output path and connectors.

steps.q: Does the affected circuit fuse have voltage on both test points?
steps.yes: Test the appliance feed and earth return under load.
steps.no: Replace only with the specified rating and investigate the circuit if it fails again.

title: Leisure battery not charging while driving

steps.q: Does starter-battery voltage rise with the engine running?
steps.no: Investigate alternator output, belt, wiring and base-vehicle charging faults.

steps.q: Does the VB04 D+ circuit become active with the engine running?
steps.no: Check the 5 A D+ fuse and trace the D+ signal.

steps.q: Does leisure-battery voltage rise with the engine running?
steps.yes: Charging is present. Assess current and voltage drop if charging remains weak.
steps.no: Inspect split-charge control, EVS charging path, connectors and cable voltage drop.

steps.q: Does the trip occur with all motorhome MCBs switched off?
steps.yes: Stop using the hook-up lead. Inspect the lead, inlet and supply polarity or earth fault.

steps.q: Does one MCB reproduce the trip when switched on?
steps.no: Reconnect loads individually and inspect portable appliances.

steps.q: Does unplugging appliances on that circuit stop the trip?
steps.yes: The appliance or lead is the likely fault. Remove it from service.
steps.no: A fixed wiring or built-in appliance fault needs professional electrical testing.
